matches.phene_name: Coat colour, mask (melanistic mask, Em allele)
matches.mol_gen: Homozygous M264V (Em/Em): all dogs with a melanistic mask carry at least one copy of this MC1R variant. Schmutz et al. (2003) showed this A>G transition at codon 264 causes the mask phenotype. Homozygous dogs express a full melanistic mask.
matches.clinical_note: Coat colour trait, not a disease variant. Consistent with a dark facial mask phenotype.

all_results.phene_name: Coat colour, mask (melanistic mask, Em allele)
all_results.mol_gen: Homozygous M264V (Em/Em): all dogs with a melanistic mask carry at least one copy of this MC1R variant. Schmutz et al. (2003) showed this A>G transition at codon 264 causes the mask phenotype. Homozygous dogs express a full melanistic mask.
all_results.clinical_note: Coat colour trait, not a disease variant. Consistent with a dark facial mask phenotype.
No disease phenotype associated in OMIA. IGF1 antisense RNA locus; variant marked as non-deleterious.

method: Exact position+allele match from OMIA database, genotyped from BAM (min depth 2, at ~3x WGS coverage)
